acceptance.command: Run the M1 authenticated local-monitoring scenario and inspect accepted/rejected Manifold-aligned receipts.
acceptance.proof: At least two simulated devices and the final bounded Quest checkpoint use an authenticated, replay-resistant, revisioned Wi-Fi check-in route with no ADB or File Manager dependency in the base profile.

acceptance.command: Run Rusty Quest Fleet Agent contract/static tests and the final serial-scoped device smoke.
acceptance.proof: The opt-in Quest agent reports battery, charging, its own lifecycle, participating-app evidence when explicitly supplied, and platform-limited foreground evidence with distinct owners, timestamps, freshness, support, authorization, and effective profile receipts.

acceptance.command: Run the M1 deterministic multi-device reconnect, sleep/wake, route-loss, replay, rotation, and upgrade scenarios.
acceptance.proof: Multiple devices check in, age, go stale/offline, reconnect, rotate keys, reject replay/stale revisions, and recover independently without one device or adapter incident overwriting another.

acceptance.command: Run the WPF model, UI Automation, keyboard, high-contrast, scaling, virtualization, and 1,000-device dependency-spike gates.
acceptance.proof: A native WPF DataGrid plus persistent inspector projects independent status families, visible scope, stable ordering, preserved selection/context, and explicit unsupported/unavailable/unauthorized/stale/degraded states at 1,000 simulated devices.

acceptance.command: Run M1 CLI/local-API/WPF projection parity tests.
acceptance.proof: Fleet Console, fleetctl, and the local API consume the same canonical query, saved-view, row, inspector, detail, and watch projections with no hidden WPF-only state.

acceptance.command: Run M1 damaged-input, backpressure, restart, degraded-adapter, and cleanup scenarios.
acceptance.proof: Transport loss, Hub restart, invalid signatures, key expiry, slow senders, queue pressure, malformed observations, adapter failure, and optional-LSL absence remain bounded, visible, and do not remove unaffected monitoring.

acceptance.command: Run the M1 authority-boundary and feature-absence checks.
acceptance.proof: The base product works without Rusty LSL; public support does not exceed its promoted main; an optional adapter cannot become identity, enrollment, or command authority.

acceptance.command: Run the declared M1 Standard gate, then the final serial-scoped device gate, then the Deep consolidation gate.
acceptance.proof: Quick checks cover local edits; Standard covers the complete host/source/UI stack; the expensive device gate runs once after host acceptance and records exact revisions, serial, APK identity, source/effective receipts, bounded logs, cleanup, and zero relevant fatals.

commit_policy: Use a small number of coherent internal checkpoints: authority/contracts plus damaged fixtures; Hub/transport/CLI parity; Quest agent plus host evidence; WPF vertical slice plus accessibility/scale evidence; then one cross-repository Standard checkpoint and one final device/deep acceptance checkpoint. Do not create file-sized or field-sized commits, run the device suite after each edit, or publish partial support claims.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the M1 Hub transport, WPF shell, Quest-agent adapter, optional-LSL boundary, and tiered validation entrypoints.
Expose the local no-ADB monitoring setup, truthful support boundary, operator entrypoints, and exact owner pins.
Register the M1 edit, Quick, Standard, UI/accessibility, performance, and bounded device gates.
Route the opt-in Fleet Agent app, platform observation limits, Manifold authority boundary, package identity, and device gate.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the focused Fleet Agent static and host-build gates in the isolated Rusty Quest worktree.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Document the Fleet Agent profile, permissions, effective receipts, build route, and explicit unsupported foreground cases.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the focused Fleet Agent static and host-build gates in the isolated Rusty Quest worktree.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether the landed Fleet/Quest product boundary changes repo-family routing; retain unchanged when the current dedicated-product guidance remains sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and record the result before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the authenticated observation, authority, state-machine, cleanup, and operator-projection lessons after implementation.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and record the result before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the new Rust, Android, and WPF source roots plus focused validation surfaces.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run a bounded tracked-file and command-surface review before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the serial-scoped APK install, launch, log, screenshot, cleanup, and effective-runtime receipt route after the bounded device checkpoint.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and record the device-policy result before acceptance.

non_scope: Participating-app or Kiosk mutation, batch command dispatch, ADB, File Manager, privileged utilities, and app installation as an operator feature.
Media capture, preview, recording, relay, FFmpeg execution, WebRTC, SRT, RIST, QUIC media, ZeroMQ payloads, or high-rate LSL samples.
Using LSL discovery or source_id as device enrollment, authentication, command authority, or the required base status route.
Depending on the unpublished Rusty LSL P68 candidate or claiming scientific interoperability before its public promotion and exact XDF compatibility gate.
Changing Manifold authority contracts, the active Rusty Quest spatial-camera worktree, unrelated Quest applications, or existing package identities.
Treating platform-limited foreground observations as authoritative for arbitrary applications.
Separate lifecycle units for individual schemas, fields, controls, fixtures, tests, documentation edits, or repairs discovered inside this milestone.
objective: Deliver Milestone 1 as one coherent local-monitoring stack: an opt-in, permission-minimal Rusty Quest Fleet Agent publishes authenticated, bounded, low-rate owner-effective status over Wi-Fi without ADB; Manifold peer status remains the enrollment, freshness, replay, revision, and admission authority; Fleet Hub retains accepted state and exposes canonical query/watch APIs; fleetctl and a native WPF fleet table plus persistent inspector project identical current, stale, offline, restricted, unsupported, and degraded outcomes; reconnect, key rotation, source limits, and cleanup are deterministic; and one bounded Quest checkpoint runs only after source, build, simulator, UI-automation, and accessibility gates pass.
outputs: Accepted M1 authority and threat-model ADRs plus exact Manifold and optional-LSL compatibility record.
Versioned authenticated check-in, enrollment/key-rotation, Quest observation, transport, reconnect, and cleanup contracts with valid, boundary, replay, stale, damaged, and degraded fixtures.
Opt-in permission-minimal Rusty Quest Fleet Agent profile, Android package, effective receipts, and focused host/device validation.
Fleet Hub local monitoring adapter, bounded state persistence, canonical local API, and fleetctl parity.
Native WPF Fleet Console table and persistent inspector with accessibility, stable-ordering, and 1,000-device performance evidence.
Sanitized Standard, device, and Deep validation receipts plus exact repository revisions and cleanup evidence.

read_only_dependencies.purpose: Consume the exact accepted low-rate peer identity, status proposal, revision, replay, TTL, enrollment, and application-receipt authority without duplicating it in Fleet or Quest.
read_only_dependencies.verification: Pin and test against commit 5c3679b5b7faaacfe65daecfaf48d442af279870; reject schema or behavior drift until explicitly reviewed.

read_only_dependencies.purpose: Track the optional scientific compatibility path without making it the base Fleet check-in transport or presenting an unpublished candidate as promoted support.
read_only_dependencies.verification: Public main remains the support boundary. Re-evaluate the green P68 candidate d0782ea5a79bf88ef16b5d0adb8792803d5705ea only after a reviewed public merge/release and exact API qualification.
